features.summary: AMD delivered record Q4 2021 results with revenue of $4.8 billion, up 49% year-over-year, marking their sixth consecutive quarter of 45%+ growth. Full year revenue reached $16.4 billion, up 68%, with gross margin expanding to 48%. Both Computing & Graphics and Enterprise segments showed strong performance, with data center revenue more than doubling. The company provided strong 2022 guidance of approximately $21.5 billion revenue (31% growth) and expects growth across all business segments.
features.management_outlook: Management expects continued strong growth in 2022 driven by robust demand across all segments, with server growth leading the way. They anticipate Q1 2022 revenue of approximately $5 billion (45% year-over-year growth) and full year gross margin of 51%. The company has made significant supply chain investments to support growth and expects the Xilinx acquisition to close in Q1 2022. Management sees data center contributing an increasing percentage of total revenue and remains confident in long-term market share gains.
features.risks: Supply chain constraints remain a key challenge, though the company has invested heavily in capacity expansion. Competitive pressures in the server market are intensifying with Intel's upcoming Sapphire Rapids platform launch. Pricing pressures from supply chain cost increases could impact margins. The company faces execution risk in ramping new 5nm Zen 4 products and RDNA 3 GPUs in the second half of 2022.
features.key_drivers: Strong demand for EPYC server processors driving data center growth, with revenue more than doubling year-over-year and expansion across cloud, enterprise, and HPC customers. Client computing benefiting from premium notebook demand and upcoming Ryzen 6000 series launch with 200+ design wins. Graphics revenue more than doubled for the third consecutive quarter, driven by RDNA 2 desktop GPUs and data center GPU wins. Semi-custom console business continues outpacing prior generations with strong Microsoft and Sony demand.
